query: What does the Fear special rule do?
rubric: Fear forces enemy units to take a Leadership test before charging or when engaged if the Fear-causing unit has a higher Unit Strength; failure prevents the charge.

query: How does Regeneration interact with Flaming Attacks?
rubric: Flaming Attacks cancel Regeneration: a model with Regeneration cannot use Regeneration saves against Flaming Attacks.

query: What happens when a unit with Stubborn takes a Break test?
rubric: The first time a Stubborn unit must take a Break test it may choose to automatically Fall Back in Good Order instead, even if outnumbered.

query: Can a unit with Fly charge over enemy units?
rubric: Yes; a flying model can move over other models and terrain, so it can charge over enemy units.

query: What is the maximum rank bonus for Heavy Infantry?
rubric: Heavy Infantry requires 4 models per rank and can claim a maximum Rank Bonus of +2.

query: How many models for a rank bonus in Swarms?
rubric: The troop-type table lists '-' for both Models per Rank and Maximum Rank Bonus for Swarms (source: tow.whfb.app/troop-types-in-detail/swarms), so Swarms cannot claim a Rank Bonus at all.

query: What unit strength does Monstrous Cavalry have?
rubric: Monstrous Cavalry has Unit Strength 3 per model.

query: How does the Magic phase work?
rubric: In the Magic phase, Wizards generate Power and Dispel dice, cast spells from their chosen Lores, and the opponent attempts to dispel them.

query: What's the difference between Magic Missile and Magical Vortex?
rubric: Magic Missiles are direct shooting attacks requiring line of sight; Magical Vortexes use templates placed on the battlefield and may remain or scatter.

query: Can you dispel a Remains in Play spell in your own magic phase?
rubric: No; you can only attempt to dispel an opponent's Remains in Play spell during your Conjuration sub-phase, not your own Magic phase.

query: What happens when a unit is Disrupted?
rubric: A Disrupted unit cannot claim a Rank Bonus; disruption is caused by flank/rear charges of sufficient Unit Strength or by terrain.

query: How do Cannon balls bounce?
rubric: After rolling for the strike point, roll another Artillery dice for bounce distance; the cannonball travels in a straight line and can hit models under its path.

query: How does the Invocation of Nehek special rule work?
rubric: Invocation of Nehek (special-rules/invocation-of-nehek) is a Tomb Kings character ability, not a spell -- it has no Casting Value. In their Command sub-phase, if not in combat, the character may attempt a Leadership test to resurrect Wounds on a friendly Necromantic Undead unit within 12", with the amount recovered scaled by the unit's troop type and the character's Level of Wizardry.

query: What spells are in the Lore of Necromancy?
rubric: List the spells belonging to the Lore of Necromancy, such as Invocation of Nehek, Raise Dead, etc.

query: Does a shield give +1 to armour save?
rubric: Yes; a shield improves Armour Value by 1 (e.g. Heavy Armour 5+ plus shield becomes 4+).

query: What is the armour value of full plate armour?
rubric: Full plate armour has an Armour Value of 4+.

query: How does the Ethereal special rule work?
rubric: Ethereal models treat all terrain as open ground, can only be wounded by Magical Attacks, and cannot join or be joined by non-Ethereal characters.

query: Does Ethereal ignore dangerous terrain?
rubric: Yes; Ethereal creatures treat all terrain, including dangerous terrain, as open ground for movement.

query: Can Scouts deploy in woods?
rubric: Yes; Scouts can deploy anywhere more than 12" from an enemy model, including inside woods, if the terrain rules allow.

query: What are the rules for Open Ground?
rubric: Open ground is any terrain not classed otherwise and has no movement or combat effects.

query: What terrain disrupts units?
rubric: Units become Disrupted if a quarter or more of their models end movement in difficult terrain or straddling a low linear obstacle.

query: Is cover granted by woods partial or full?
rubric: Woods can grant partial or full cover depending on how much of the target is obscured; partial cover gives -1 To Hit, full cover gives -2.

query: What's the FAQ about rerolls being discarded?
rubric: Answer should state the specific FAQ ruling about rerolls being discarded, or clearly say the archive does not contain it.

query: What errata changed the Steam Tank rules?
rubric: Answer should identify the errata entry affecting the Steam Tank and what changed, or clearly state the archive does not contain it.

query: What version is the latest FAQ from?
rubric: Answer should state the version/date of the latest FAQ document, or clearly say the archive does not contain that metadata.

query: Build me a 2000pt Vampire Counts list
rubric: Answer should propose a Vampire Counts army list totalling roughly 2000 points with Lords/Heroes/Core/Special/Rare categories and cite the units/items used.

query: What units are available in the Empire of Man?
rubric: Answer should name several Empire of Man units drawn from the army list and cite them.

query: What special rules do Blood Knights have?
rubric: Answer should list the special rules of Blood Knights and cite the unit.

query: How much do Blood Knights cost per model?
rubric: Blood Knights cost 39 points per model.

query: What's the base size of a Steam Tank?
rubric: The Empire Steam Tank is on a 60x100mm base.

query: What equipment do Grave Guard come with by default?
rubric: Answer should state Grave Guard's default equipment (e.g. Great Weapon, Heavy Armour) and note shield options.

query: What upgrades can I give to Skeleton Warriors?
rubric: Answer should list upgrade options available to Vampire Counts Skeleton Warriors (e.g. spears, shields, champion, standard, musician) and cite the unit.

query: How much to give shields to 20 Clanrats?
rubric: Answer should calculate the points cost for giving shields to 20 Clanrats and cite the unit/upgrade.

query: Can a Vampire Lord take magic items? How many points?
rubric: A Vampire Count can take magic items; the exact points limit is listed in the model's options (commonly up to 100 points).

query: What magic items can a Tomb King take?
rubric: A Tomb King can take magic items from the Tomb Kings magic items list up to the points limit in its options.

query: Does my Wizard have access to Lore of Necromancy?
rubric: Answer should explain which armies/characters can use the Lore of Necromancy and that a generic Wizard cannot unless allowed by its army list.

query: What mount options does a Chaos Lord have?
rubric: Answer should list the mount options available to a Chaos Lord (e.g. Chaos Steed, Daemonic Mount) and cite the unit.

query: How much does a barded Nightmare cost as a mount?
rubric: Answer should state the points cost of a barded Nightmare mount option and cite the source.

query: What's the profile of a Zombie Dragon?
rubric: Answer should provide the Zombie Dragon's profile stats (M, WS, BS, S, T, W, I, A, Ld) and cite the unit.

query: Can I take a Battle Standard Bearer?
rubric: Yes, a Battle Standard Bearer is an option in most army lists, carried by a character who cannot be the General.

query: What percentage of my army can be Characters?
rubric: Characters can account for up to 50% of the army's points in a Grand Army composition list.

query: Which armies can ally with Vampire Counts?
rubric: Answer should list the armies that can be taken as allies by Vampire Counts according to its composition list.

query: Difference between trusted and suspicious allies?
rubric: Trusted/Uneasy allies follow normal allied rules with a Levies penalty; Suspicious allies add extra restrictions such as causing Panic when fleeing through main-army units.

query: How many points of magic items can my champion take?
rubric: Only some champions have the option to purchase magic items; the points limit is given in the unit's options, not a universal value.

query: Can a unit standard bearer take a magic standard?
rubric: Yes, if the unit's options allow it, a standard bearer can take a Magic Standard (a type of magic item).

query: What Great Weapons does this unit swap to?
rubric: Answer should ask which unit is meant, or explain that Great Weapon swaps are unit-specific options.

query: What troops can I bring as Mercenaries?
rubric: Answer should explain that Mercenaries are units drawn from another army list as allowed by the army's composition list and list the restrictions.

query: What's the split profile for a Demigryph Knight?
rubric: Answer should explain that Demigryph Knights use a split profile (rider + Demigryph) and give both profile lines.

query: What's a Varghulf's WS and attacks?
rubric: Answer should state the Varghulf's Weapon Skill and Attacks values from its profile and cite the unit.

query: Is a named character required to include its unit?
rubric: No; named characters are independent choices and are only required to include a unit if their own rules explicitly say so.

query: What special rules does a Great Weapon give in combat?
rubric: A Great Weapon requires two hands and grants Armour Bane (1); it gives Strength +2 and AP -2, but the wielder Strikes Last.

query: Does a Halberd get better armour penetration when charging?
rubric: Yes; a Halberd normally has AP -1, but its notes give it AP -2 against enemy models the wielder charged that turn.

query: How many points does the Dragon Slaying Sword cost?
rubric: The Dragon Slaying Sword is a 50-point magic weapon.

query: What type of magic item is the Spelleater Axe and what does it cost?
rubric: The Spelleater Axe is a magic weapon costing 35 points.

query: My unit has a quarter of its models stuck in difficult terrain -- does that mess with my rank bonus?
rubric: Yes; a unit that ends its movement or begins a Combat phase with a quarter (25%) or more of its models within difficult terrain becomes Disrupted and cannot claim a Rank Bonus.

query: Can I draw line of sight through a wood if neither model is standing inside it?
rubric: No; per Arboreal Gloom, if a wood lies between two models and neither is within the wood, line of sight can only be drawn if an uninterrupted line can be drawn without crossing over or through the wood.

query: What counts as impassable terrain?
rubric: Impassable terrain completely blocks movement -- rock spires, deep rivers, sheer cliffs, most buildings, and high linear obstacles like castle walls; units must go around it, and a charging unit that can't align due to impassable terrain makes a disordered charge.

query: How much terrain should I set up on a table with a 72 inch long edge?
rubric: The guideline is one terrain feature per 12 inches of the longest table edge, rounded up -- for a 72-inch edge that's 6 features.

query: Was there an errata to the Skeleton Chariots' points cost?
rubric: Yes; the errata changes Skeleton Chariots' points to 41 and their Armour Value to 4+.

query: What did the errata change about Minotaurs?
rubric: The errata changes Minotaurs' points cost to 44.

query: Can a Steam Tank's steam cannon fire grapeshot?
rubric: Yes; grapeshot can be fired by any cannon, i.e. any weapon that fires using the Cannon Fire special rule.

query: If I re-roll a dice, does the original roll's triggered effect still count?
rubric: No; when a dice is re-rolled, the previous roll is discarded, including any effects that roll may have triggered.

query: Do my mercenaries get my army's special rules?
rubric: Unless stated otherwise, no -- army special rules don't extend to included mercenaries.

query: Can an Archmage ride a Star Dragon into battle?
rubric: Yes; the Star Dragon is one of the Archmage's available mount options.

query: Is a Lothern Skycutter a valid mount option for an Archmage?
rubric: Yes; the Lothern Skycutter is one of the Archmage's mount options.

query: Can an Arch-Necrotect be equipped with the Warding Splint?
rubric: Yes; the Warding Splint (a 35-point magic armour) is available to the Arch-Necrotect and grants a 5+ Ward save.

query: My knights are just passing through a swamp, not stopping there -- are they still at risk?
rubric: Yes; a Dangerous Terrain test is required whether a model begins its movement in, passes through, or ends its movement in dangerous terrain, not only if it stops there.

query: If my Unbreakable unit loses combat badly, does it get destroyed?
rubric: No; a unit with Unbreakable that loses a round of combat is not required to make a Break test -- it automatically Gives Ground instead.

query: I want to charge through a hedge -- will that slow my unit down?
rubric: Yes; a hedge under 2 inches high is a low linear obstacle. A charge through any difficult/dangerous terrain or low linear obstacle forces the charging unit to discard the highest Charge roll die (instead of the lowest) and suffer -1 Movement (to a minimum of 1) for that charge move.

query: I've got 500 points to spend on Core in a 2000-point Grand Army -- is that enough?
rubric: Yes, exactly enough -- a Grand Army composition list requires at least 25% of points on Core, and 25% of 2000 is 500.

query: Half my unit just got shot to bits -- do they turn and run straight away?
rubric: Not automatically -- the unit makes a Panic test against its Leadership. If it fails with more than 50% of its starting models remaining it Falls Back in Good Order; with 50% or fewer remaining it flees.

query: My frenzied warriors have a charge available but I'd rather hold them back -- can I?
rubric: No; a unit with one or more Frenzied models that can declare a charge must do so. Frenzied models also get +1 Attacks during a turn they charged (or the turn after a follow up move).

query: My trolls failed their Stupidity test -- what can they actually still do this turn?
rubric: Very little: a unit that succumbs to Stupidity cannot move (except to flee), cannot shoot or cast spells, cannot attempt a Wizardly dispel, and must Hold if charged.

query: A terror-causing dragon just declared a charge on my spearmen -- what do I roll before anything else?
rubric: The charged unit immediately makes a Leadership test: if failed it must Flee; if passed it declares its charge reaction normally. (If the unit cannot choose to Flee, no test is made.)

query: Why do Lizardmen shrug off fear and panic so easily?
rubric: Cold Blooded: when making a Fear, Panic or Terror test, the model rolls an extra D6 and discards the highest result.

query: The chargers started really close to my archers -- can they still stand and shoot?
rubric: No; if the distance between the units is less than the charging unit's Movement characteristic, the charged unit cannot Stand & Shoot and must Hold or Flee instead. (If far enough, it may shoot even if the charger is beyond weapon range, then Holds.)

query: What do I need to roll to get my fleeing unit back in the fight?
rubric: A Rally test -- during the Rally Fleeing Units sub-phase of the Strategy phase, test against the fleeing unit's Leadership; pass and it rallies, fail and it continues fleeing.

query: Can units near my general borrow his Leadership?
rubric: Yes; Inspiring Presence lets friendly units within the General's Command range use the General's Leadership instead of their own, unless the General is fleeing.

query: What re-rolls does my Battle Standard Bearer actually hand out?
rubric: Hold Your Ground: friendly units within the BSB's Command range may re-roll failed Panic or Rally tests (unless the BSB is fleeing), and may re-roll the 2D6 of a Break test, accepting the second result even if worse.

query: Does armour piercing chew through ward saves too?
rubric: No; rules that affect armour values (like Armour Piercing) do not affect Warding values unless explicitly stated otherwise.

query: I rolled a 6 to wound with my Killing Blow guy -- what happens to that knight?
rubric: A natural 6 To Wound in combat strikes a Killing Blow: infantry or cavalry victims get no armour or Regeneration save (Ward saves are allowed), and an unsaved wound makes the model lose all its remaining Wounds.

query: Do poisoned attacks auto-wound when I roll a 6 to hit?
rubric: No auto-wound; a natural 6 To Hit lets the model apply a +2 modifier to that hit's roll To Wound. It doesn't work on spells, magic weapon attacks, attacks needing 7+ To Hit, or automatic hits.

query: Do my chariot's impact hits need to roll to hit?
rubric: No; Impact Hits hit automatically, using the model's unmodified Strength. They require the charger to have moved 3" or more into base contact, and are resolved when the combat is chosen, before challenges are issued.

query: Can my dragon breathe fire at chargers as a stand and shoot reaction?
rubric: No; a Breath Weapon can be used once per round during the model's own Shooting phase, and cannot be used as a Stand & Shoot reaction or while engaged in combat.

query: What do lances actually do for my knights on the charge?
rubric: A lance gives Strength +2 and AP -2, but only during a turn in which the wielder charged and only against models it charged; usable only by cavalry or monster troop types.

query: How far does a handgun shoot and how hard does it hit?
rubric: A handgun has Range 24", Strength 4, and AP -1.

query: If my archers fire multiple shots, what's the accuracy penalty?
rubric: Each roll To Hit suffers an additional -1 modifier when firing Multiple Shots, and all models in the unit must fire the same way (all single or all multiple).

query: What kicks in when I roll a 6 to wound with an Armour Bane weapon?
rubric: On a natural 6 To Wound, the weapon's Armour Piercing characteristic is improved by the bracketed X value (e.g. AP '-' with Armour Bane (1) counts as AP -1 for that wound).

query: I rolled snake eyes on my casting roll -- how bad is it?
rubric: A natural double 1 on a Casting roll is a miscast: the spell is (unless stated otherwise) not cast and you roll 2D6 on the Miscast table -- the worst result (2-4, Dimensional Cascade) centres a 5" template on the Wizard dealing Strength 10, AP -4 hits.

query: Is a Dispel Scroll a one-shot thing, and what does it actually do?
rubric: Yes, single use: when attempting a Wizardly dispel the Wizard rolls an extra D6 and discards the lowest result; but if a double 1 comes up on any two dice, the Wizard is outclassed.

query: Can my general dodge a cannonball by hiding in a unit?
rubric: If the character is hit by a shooting attack while in a unit with five or more rank and file models of the same troop type, roll a D6 for "Look Out, Sir!": on a 2+ a unit member is hit instead; on a 1 the character takes the hit.

query: How far can my troops march, and can they still shoot afterwards?
rubric: A marching unit doubles its Movement (a Close Order formation in Marching Column may triple it) and may only wheel; a unit that marched cannot shoot that turn, including casting Magic Missile or Magical Vortex spells.

query: What does Swiftstride add to my cavalry's charge range?
rubric: A unit made up entirely of Swiftstride models increases its maximum possible charge range by 3" and may choose to apply a +D6 modifier before making a Charge, Flee or Pursuit roll.

query: I wiped the enemy out on the charge -- can I keep rolling forward into the next unit?
rubric: If a unit completely destroys its enemy before the Break Test sub-phase it may overrun: a normal pursuit move that must go directly forwards, without pivoting (or it may attempt to restrain and reform).

query: What happens to my hero if he chickens out of a challenge?
rubric: If a challenge is refused, the issuing player nominates one of the eligible characters/champions, who must retire from combat: it moves away from the fighting rank, cannot attack or be attacked, and confers no benefits (Leadership, special rules or otherwise) to its unit.

query: My daemons lost combat by 3 -- what do they roll and what's the worst case?
rubric: A Leadership test with +1 to the result per combat result point lost (here +3): if failed, the unit loses Wounds equal to the amount it failed by; on a natural double 6 the unit is dragged back into the aether and removed from play.

query: When can my Orc warboss call a Waaagh and what does everyone get?
rubric: Once per game, during the Command sub-phase, the character passes a Leadership test: until your next Start of Turn sub-phase the character, their mount and any joined Orc unit re-roll To Hit rolls of a natural 1 and claim +1 combat result point.

query: What does my Bretonnian army trade away for the Lady's blessing?
rubric: Instead of rolling off for first turn, the Bretonnian army kneels and prays -- the opponent counts as winning the roll-off. In exchange, models with the rule get a 6+ Ward save against any wounds, improved to 5+ against wounds from Strength 5 or higher attacks.

query: What am I paying for Grail Knights and what do they bring to the table?
rubric: Grail Knights cost 38 points per model (unit size 3+), with WS6 S4 T4 A2 Ld9, and carry rules including Blessings of the Lady, Lance Formation, First Charge, Counter Charge and Swiftstride.

query: Are Saurus Warriors any good for their points?
rubric: Saurus Warriors cost 14 points per model (unit size 10+), with M4 WS3 S4 T4 A2 Ld8 and the Close Order, Cold Blooded and Obsidian Blades rules -- durable, hard-hitting core infantry.
